name: Indian Pudding
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 300°F.
2. Butter a small casserole dish (6 1/2 inches x 10 1/2 inches).
3. In a large saucepan, heat the milk over medium heat until it is hot but not boiling.
4. Slowly whisk in the cornmeal and continue to stir until the mixture begins to thicken, about 10 minutes.
5. Add the remaining ingredients (maple syrup, molasses, ginger, cinnamon, salt, raisins) and keep stirring until everything is heated through.
6. Pour the mixture into the prepared casserole dish.
7. Bake for 3 hours, or until the sides are browned and sticky-looking. The pudding will firm up slightly as it cools.
8. Serve warm with a generous scoop of vanilla ice cream.
This comforting New England dessert is perfect for wintertime.
